title: Benefits and Services Assistance * Immigration Issues - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Benefits and Services Assistance * Immigration Issues 2 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - Disabilities Issues Immigration Issues Native American Community Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF CATHOLIC CHARITIES REFUGEE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides comprehensive services for newly arrived refugees, asylees, Special Immigrant Visa (SIV) holders, foreign born victims of trafficking, or Cuban/Haitian entrants. Program staff and community volunteers work to meet refugees' basic needs, including shelter, furnishings, food, and clothing. Case Managers facilitate referrals to other service providers and encourage refugee newcomers towards self-sufficiency through obtaining safe and secure housing, cultural orientation classes, applying for health insurance, employment services, English language classes, public assistance, and school enrollment. 2740 SE Powell Boulevard #5 Portland, OR 97202 Eligibility: Must have documentation present confirming their status (Refugee, Asylum-seeker, Asylee, SIV, foreign born victims of trafficking, or Cuban or Haitian entrant) and must have been in the US for less than 60 months from date of status granted. Most services can be rendered up to 2 years in total. Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (971) 222-1883 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON PORTLAND REGIONAL OFFICE PUBLIC BENEFITS HOTLINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides advice and representation to individuals facing issues with government benefits and public charge questions, including Social Security, Medicare, Medicaid/Oregon Health Plan (OHP), Coordinated Care Organization (CCO) service denials or other CCO-related problems, medical billing and debt collection issues, Temporary Assistance for Needy Families (TANF), refugee benefits, SNAP (food stamps), overpayments, in-home services, unemployment compensation, Employment Related Day Care (ERDC), and Temporary Assistance for Domestic Violence Survivors (TADVS). Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday/Thursday 1pm-4pm, Tuesday/Wednesday 9:30am-12:30pm (800) 520-5292 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CATHOLIC CHARITIES REFUGEE SERVICES
